breaking_news.title: Interlock ransomware exploits Cisco firewall zero-day weeks before patch
breaking_news.summary:
The Interlock ransomware group exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software weeks before a patch was released. Amazon Threat Intelligence discovered that attackers used CVE-2026-20131 to gain root access via insecure Java deserialization since late January.

CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, urging immediate remediation across global networks. This incident highlights the severe risk of unpatched firewall infrastructure and the aggressive tactics of modern ransomware operators targeting critical security perimeters.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS exploit kit uses zero-days for state-sponsored attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Google Threat Intelligence identified a new iOS exploit kit named DarkSword that leverages six vulnerabilities, including three zero-days, to achieve full device compromise. State-sponsored actors and commercial spyware vendors have deployed this chain against targets in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine since late 2025.

Apple has issued warnings for older iPhone versions, emphasizing the need for updates to prevent drive-by infections via malicious websites. The proliferation of this sophisticated toolkit demonstrates an escalating threat to mobile privacy and the increasing capability of surveillance vendors to bypass iOS security controls.

breaking_news.title: Handala group wipes Stryker devices via Microsoft Intune abuse
breaking_news.summary:
Pro-Iranian threat actor Handala conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker, wiping approximately 80,000 devices without using traditional malware. The group abused Microsoft Intune endpoint management configurations to remotely erase data and disrupt operations across 79 countries.

CISA and the FBI have issued urgent advisories urging organizations to harden their Intune systems following the seizure of Handala’s data leak sites. This attack underscores the critical vulnerability of cloud-based device management tools and the potential for significant operational disruption in the healthcare sector.

breaking_news.title: APT28 exploits Zimbra flaw in attacks against Ukrainian government
breaking_news.summary:
Russian military intelligence group APT28 is actively exploiting a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite to target Ukrainian government entities. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-66376, allows attackers to execute scripts via HTML emails and compromise user accounts within the email environment.

CISA has added this vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, mandating patches for U.S. federal agencies. This campaign illustrates the continued use of legacy collaboration software as a primary vector for state-sponsored espionage against critical government infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Critical Microsoft SharePoint flaw actively exploited in attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Unknown attackers are actively exploiting a critical deserialization vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint servers to execute arbitrary code remotely. CISA has flagged CVE-2026-20963 as a known exploited vulnerability, noting that unauthenticated attackers can inject and run code over the network.

This widespread exploitation affects numerous enterprises relying on SharePoint for document management and collaboration services. Security teams must prioritize patching immediately to prevent unauthorized access and potential data exfiltration from internal corporate networks.

breaking_news.title: DoJ disrupts 3 million-device IoT botnets behind record DDoS attacks
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Department of Justice, alongside Canadian and German authorities, disrupted command-and-control infrastructure for four major IoT botnets compromising over three million devices. These botnets, including AISURU and Kimwolf, were responsible for record-breaking DDoS attacks capable of knocking major targets offline.

Law enforcement executed seizure warrants against domains and servers used to launch hundreds of thousands of attacks against internet addresses owned by the Department of Defense. This operation significantly reduces the immediate threat landscape for critical infrastructure but highlights the persistent risk of insecure IoT devices.

breaking_news.title: Critical unpatched Telnetd flaw enables unauthenticated root RCE
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers disclosed a critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in GNU InetUtils telnetd with a maximum CVSS score of 9.8. Tracked as CVE-2026-32746, the flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code as root via a buffer overflow in the LINEMODE handler.

This vulnerability affects legacy infrastructure and embedded systems that still rely on the Telnet protocol instead of secure alternatives like SSH. Organizations must audit their networks for vulnerable telnetd instances immediately, as no patch is available until early April 2026.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm malware targets 400+ code repos on GitHub and npm
breaking_news.summary:
The GlassWorm supply-chain campaign has targeted hundreds of packages and repositories across GitHub, npm, and VSCode extensions with malicious code. This coordinated attack aims to compromise developer environments and steal sensitive credentials from software projects.

Security researchers warn that this activity represents a significant escalation in supply chain risks for open-source software dependencies. Developers must verify package integrity and monitor for unauthorized changes to prevent widespread compromise of their development pipelines.
